# AI Worker M3 Ingestion Pipeline Implementation Plan > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. **Goal:** Implement real document parsing (LiteParse), semantic text chunking, and multi-provider embedding in the Raven AI worker (M3: issues #14, #16, #17). **Architecture:** The AI worker is a Python gRPC server. The three processors (`DocumentParser`, `TextChunker`, `EmbeddingServicer`) are stubs — replace each with a real implementation. All are invoked by the `ParseAndEmbed` RPC in `server.py`. Add missing dependencies to `pyproject.toml` before implementing. TDD with pytest-asyncio. **Tech Stack:** Python 3.12, grpcio, liteparse, langchain-text-splitters (chunking), openai SDK, pytest, pytest-asyncio. **Worktree:** `.claude/worktrees/stream-ai` on branch `feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion` --- ## Pre-flight: Read before writing a single line - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/services/embedding.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/base.py` — understand provider interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/openai_provider.py` — understand existing OpenAI stub - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/server.py` — understand how processors are wired to RPCs - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/config.py` — understand config structure - [ ] Read `ai-worker/tests/conftest.py` — understand test setup and fixtures - [ ] Read `ai-worker/pyproject.toml` — check what dependencies exist; liteparse is NOT there yet - [ ] Read `proto/ai_worker.proto` — understand `ParseRequest`/`ParseResponse` contract --- ## Task 1: Add dependencies - [ ] Add to `ai-worker/pyproject.toml` under `[project] dependencies`: ```toml "liteparse>=0.3.0", "langchain-text-splitters>=0.3.0", "openai>=1.58.0", "tiktoken>=0.8.0", "httpx>=0.28.0", ``` - [ ] Install in worktree: ```bash cd .claude/worktrees/stream-ai/ai-worker python -m pip install -e ".[dev]" 2>&1 | tail -5 ``` - [ ] Verify liteparse is importable: ```bash python -c "import liteparse; print('liteparse ok')" ``` - [ ] Verify langchain splitters: ```bash python -c "from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter; print('splitters ok')" ``` - [ ] Commit: ```bash git add ai-worker/pyproject.toml git commit -m "deps(ai-worker): add liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai, tiktoken" ``` --- ## Task 2: Issue #14 — LiteParse Integration (DocumentParser) **GitHub issue:** #14 — Replace `NotImplementedError` stub with real LiteParse parsing. **Files:** - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py` - Create: `ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py` **Supported formats:** PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, Markdown, plain text. - [ ] Write failing tests in `ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py`: ```python import pytest from raven_worker.processors.parser import DocumentParser @pytest.fixture def parser(): return DocumentParser() @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_plain_text(parser): content = b"Hello, world! This is a test document." result = await parser.parse(content, "text/plain", "test.txt") assert "Hello, world!" in result assert len(result) > 0 @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_markdown(parser): content = b"# Title\n\nSome content here.\n\n## Section\n\nMore text." result = await parser.parse(content, "text/markdown", "doc.md") assert "Title" in result assert "Some content here" in result @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_html(parser): content = b"

Title

Some content.

" result = await parser.parse(content, "text/html", "page.html") assert "Title" in result assert "Some content" in result @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_unknown_mime_raises(parser): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unsupported"): await parser.parse(b"data", "application/octet-stream", "file.bin") @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_empty_content_returns_empty(parser): result = await parser.parse(b"", "text/plain", "empty.txt") assert result == "" ``` - [ ] Run — expect FAIL: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_parser.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Implement `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py`: ```python """LiteParse integration for document parsing.""" from __future__ import annotations import asyncio import io from functools import partial import liteparse import structlog logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__) # MIME types supported by LiteParse _SUPPORTED = { "application/pdf", "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document", # docx "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation", # pptx "text/html", "text/markdown", "text/plain", } _EXTENSION_FALLBACK: dict[str, str] = { ".pdf": "application/pdf", ".docx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document", ".pptx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation", ".html": "text/html", ".htm": "text/html", ".md": "text/markdown", ".txt": "text/plain", } class DocumentParser: """Parse documents into plain text using LiteParse.""" async def parse(self, content: bytes, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: """Extract text content from a document. Args: content: Raw document bytes. mime_type: MIME type. Falls back to extension detection if not supported. file_name: Original file name for extension-based fallback. Returns: Extracted plain text. Raises: ValueError: If the MIME type is not supported and no fallback exists. """ if not content: return "" resolved_mime = self._resolve_mime(mime_type, file_name) logger.info( "parse_document", mime_type=resolved_mime, file_name=file_name, size=len(content), ) # Run sync liteparse in thread pool to avoid blocking event loop loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() text = await loop.run_in_executor( None, partial(self._parse_sync, content, resolved_mime, file_name), ) return text.strip() def _resolve_mime(self, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: if mime_type in _SUPPORTED: return mime_type # Try extension fallback suffix = "." + file_name.rsplit(".", 1)[-1].lower() if "." in file_name else "" fallback = _EXTENSION_FALLBACK.get(suffix) if fallback: return fallback raise ValueError(f"Unsupported MIME type: {mime_type} (file: {file_name})") def _parse_sync(self, content: bytes, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: doc = liteparse.Document( content=io.BytesIO(content), mime_type=mime_type, filename=file_name, ) return doc.extract_text() ``` - [ ] Run tests — expect PASS: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_parser.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Run full test suite to confirm no regressions: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Commit: ```bash git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py git commit -m "feat(#14): implement LiteParse document parsing (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, MD, TXT)" ``` - [ ] Push and create PR: ```bash git push origin feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion gh pr create --title "feat: LiteParse document parsing (#14)" \ --body "Closes #14" ``` --- ## Task 3: Issue #16 — Text Chunking (TextChunker) **GitHub issue:** #16 — Replace basic sliding window with semantic recursive character chunking. **Files:** - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py` - Create: `ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py` **Strategy:** Use `langchain_text_splitters.RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` — splits on paragraphs, then sentences, then characters. Preserves semantic boundaries better than a fixed sliding window. - [ ] Write failing tests in `ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py`: ```python import pytest from raven_worker.processors.chunker import TextChunker def test_empty_text_returns_empty_list(): chunker = TextChunker() assert chunker.chunk("") == [] def test_short_text_returns_single_chunk(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=512, chunk_overlap=64) text = "Short text under the chunk size limit." chunks = chunker.chunk(text) assert len(chunks) == 1 assert chunks[0] == text def test_long_text_produces_multiple_chunks(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=100, chunk_overlap=20) text = "word " * 100 # 500 chars chunks = chunker.chunk(text) assert len(chunks) > 1 def test_chunks_have_overlap(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=50, chunk_overlap=10) # Build text where overlap can be detected text = "AAAA " * 20 # repeating chunks = chunker.chunk(text) # Each chunk should be <= chunk_size + some tolerance for chunk in chunks: assert len(chunk) <= 60 # chunk_size + small tolerance def test_chunk_count_matches_log_metadata(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=100, chunk_overlap=20) text = "sentence. " * 50 chunks = chunker.chunk(text) assert len(chunks) > 0 def test_custom_separators_respected(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=200, chunk_overlap=0) # Text with clear paragraph boundaries text = "Paragraph one.\n\nParagraph two.\n\nParagraph three." chunks = chunker.chunk(text) # Should split on paragraphs assert any("Paragraph one" in c for c in chunks) ``` - [ ] Run — expect some failures (current implementation may not match): ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_chunker.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Replace `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py`: ```python """Semantic text chunking using RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.""" from __future__ import annotations import structlog from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__) DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE = 512 DEFAULT_CHUNK_OVERLAP = 64 class TextChunker: """Split text into overlapping chunks suitable for embedding. Uses RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter which tries to split on natural boundaries (paragraphs → sentences → words → chars) before falling back to raw character splits. """ def __init__( self, chunk_size: int = DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE, chunk_overlap: int = DEFAULT_CHUNK_OVERLAP, ) -> None: self.chunk_size = chunk_size self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap self._splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=chunk_size, chunk_overlap=chunk_overlap, separators=["\n\n", "\n", ". ", "! ", "? ", " ", ""], ) def chunk(self, text: str) -> list[str]: """Split text into overlapping semantic chunks. Args: text: Full document text. Returns: List of text chunks, each <= chunk_size characters (approximately). """ if not text: return [] chunks = self._splitter.split_text(text) logger.info( "chunked_text", total_length=len(text), chunk_count=len(chunks), chunk_size=self.chunk_size, chunk_overlap=self.chunk_overlap, ) return chunks ``` - [ ] Run tests — expect PASS: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_chunker.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Run full test suite: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Commit: ```bash git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py git commit -m "feat(#16): semantic text chunking with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter" ``` - [ ] Push PR: ```bash gh pr create --title "feat: semantic text chunking (#16)" \ --body "Closes #16" ``` --- ## Task 4: Issue #17 — Multi-Provider Embedding (BYOK) **GitHub issue:** #17 — Real embedding via OpenAI (and extensible to other providers). **Files:** - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/base.py` — define protocol - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/openai_provider.py` — real OpenAI impl - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/services/embedding.py` — wire provider selection - # AI Worker M3 Ingestion Pipeline Implementation Plan > **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking. **Goal:** Implement real document parsing (LiteParse), semantic text chunking, and multi-provider embedding in the Raven AI worker (M3: issues #14, #16, #17). **Architecture:** The AI worker is a Python gRPC server. The three processors (`DocumentParser`, `TextChunker`, `EmbeddingServicer`) are stubs — replace each with a real implementation. All are invoked by the `ParseAndEmbed` RPC in `server.py`. Add missing dependencies to `pyproject.toml` before implementing. TDD with pytest-asyncio. **Tech Stack:** Python 3.12, grpcio, liteparse, langchain-text-splitters (chunking), openai SDK, pytest, pytest-asyncio. **Worktree:** `.claude/worktrees/stream-ai` on branch `feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion` --- ## Pre-flight: Read before writing a single line - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/services/embedding.py` — understand stub interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/base.py` — understand provider interface - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/providers/openai_provider.py` — understand existing OpenAI stub - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/server.py` — understand how processors are wired to RPCs - [ ] Read `ai-worker/raven_worker/config.py` — understand config structure - [ ] Read `ai-worker/tests/conftest.py` — understand test setup and fixtures - [ ] Read `ai-worker/pyproject.toml` — check what dependencies exist; liteparse is NOT there yet - [ ] Read `proto/ai_worker.proto` — understand `ParseRequest`/`ParseResponse` contract --- ## Task 1: Add dependencies - [ ] Add to `ai-worker/pyproject.toml` under `[project] dependencies`: ```toml "liteparse>=0.3.0", "langchain-text-splitters>=0.3.0", "openai>=1.58.0", "tiktoken>=0.8.0", "httpx>=0.28.0", ``` - [ ] Install in worktree: ```bash cd .claude/worktrees/stream-ai/ai-worker python -m pip install -e ".[dev]" 2>&1 | tail -5 ``` - [ ] Verify liteparse is importable: ```bash python -c "import liteparse; print('liteparse ok')" ``` - [ ] Verify langchain splitters: ```bash python -c "from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter; print('splitters ok')" ``` - [ ] Commit: ```bash git add ai-worker/pyproject.toml git commit -m "deps(ai-worker): add liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai, tiktoken" ``` --- ## Task 2: Issue #14 — LiteParse Integration (DocumentParser) **GitHub issue:** #14 — Replace `NotImplementedError` stub with real LiteParse parsing. **Files:** - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py` - Create: `ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py` **Supported formats:** PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, Markdown, plain text. - [ ] Write failing tests in `ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py`: ```python import pytest from raven_worker.processors.parser import DocumentParser @pytest.fixture def parser(): return DocumentParser() @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_plain_text(parser): content = b"Hello, world! This is a test document." result = await parser.parse(content, "text/plain", "test.txt") assert "Hello, world!" in result assert len(result) > 0 @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_markdown(parser): content = b"# Title\n\nSome content here.\n\n## Section\n\nMore text." result = await parser.parse(content, "text/markdown", "doc.md") assert "Title" in result assert "Some content here" in result @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_html(parser): content = b"

Title

Some content.

" result = await parser.parse(content, "text/html", "page.html") assert "Title" in result assert "Some content" in result @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_unknown_mime_raises(parser): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Unsupported"): await parser.parse(b"data", "application/octet-stream", "file.bin") @pytest.mark.asyncio async def test_parse_empty_content_returns_empty(parser): result = await parser.parse(b"", "text/plain", "empty.txt") assert result == "" ``` - [ ] Run — expect FAIL: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_parser.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Implement `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py`: ```python """LiteParse integration for document parsing.""" from __future__ import annotations import asyncio import io from functools import partial import liteparse import structlog logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__) # MIME types supported by LiteParse _SUPPORTED = { "application/pdf", "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document", # docx "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation", # pptx "text/html", "text/markdown", "text/plain", } _EXTENSION_FALLBACK: dict[str, str] = { ".pdf": "application/pdf", ".docx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document", ".pptx": "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation", ".html": "text/html", ".htm": "text/html", ".md": "text/markdown", ".txt": "text/plain", } class DocumentParser: """Parse documents into plain text using LiteParse.""" async def parse(self, content: bytes, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: """Extract text content from a document. Args: content: Raw document bytes. mime_type: MIME type. Falls back to extension detection if not supported. file_name: Original file name for extension-based fallback. Returns: Extracted plain text. Raises: ValueError: If the MIME type is not supported and no fallback exists. """ if not content: return "" resolved_mime = self._resolve_mime(mime_type, file_name) logger.info( "parse_document", mime_type=resolved_mime, file_name=file_name, size=len(content), ) # Run sync liteparse in thread pool to avoid blocking event loop loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() text = await loop.run_in_executor( None, partial(self._parse_sync, content, resolved_mime, file_name), ) return text.strip() def _resolve_mime(self, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: if mime_type in _SUPPORTED: return mime_type # Try extension fallback suffix = "." + file_name.rsplit(".", 1)[-1].lower() if "." in file_name else "" fallback = _EXTENSION_FALLBACK.get(suffix) if fallback: return fallback raise ValueError(f"Unsupported MIME type: {mime_type} (file: {file_name})") def _parse_sync(self, content: bytes, mime_type: str, file_name: str) -> str: doc = liteparse.Document( content=io.BytesIO(content), mime_type=mime_type, filename=file_name, ) return doc.extract_text() ``` - [ ] Run tests — expect PASS: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_parser.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Run full test suite to confirm no regressions: ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Commit: ```bash git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py git commit -m "feat(#14): implement LiteParse document parsing (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, MD, TXT)" ``` - [ ] Push and create PR: ```bash git push origin feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion gh pr create --title "feat: LiteParse document parsing (#14)" \ --body "Closes #14" ``` --- ## Task 3: Issue #16 — Text Chunking (TextChunker) **GitHub issue:** #16 — Replace basic sliding window with semantic recursive character chunking. **Files:** - Replace: `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py` - Create: `ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py` **Strategy:** Use `langchain_text_splitters.RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` — splits on paragraphs, then sentences, then characters. " * 50 chunks = chunker.chunk(text) assert len(chunks) > 0 def test_custom_separators_respected(): chunker = TextChunker(chunk_size=200, chunk_overlap=0) # Text with clear paragraph boundaries text = "Paragraph one.\n\nParagraph two.\n\nParagraph three." chunks = chunker.chunk(text) # Should split on paragraphs assert any("Paragraph one" in c for c in chunks) ``` - [ ] Run — expect some failures (current implementation may not match): ```bash cd ai-worker && python -m pytest tests/test_chunker.py -v 2>&1 ``` - [ ] Replace `ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py`: ```python """Semantic text chunking using RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.""" from __future__ import annotations import structlog from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__) DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE = 512 DEFAULT_CHUNK_OVERLAP = 64 class TextChunker: """Split text into overlapping chunks suitable for embedding. Uses RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter which tries to split on natural boundaries (paragraphs → sentences → words → chars) before falling back to raw character splits. """ def __init__( self, chunk_size: int = DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE, chunk_overlap: int = DEFAULT_CHUNK_OVERLAP, ) -> None: self.chunk_size = chunk_size self.chunk_overlap = chunk_overlap self._splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter( chunk_size=chunk_size, chunk_overlap=chunk_overlap, separators=["\n\n", "\n", ". ", "! ", "? ", " ", ""], ) def chunk(self, text: str) -> list[str]: """Split text into overlapping semantic chunks. Args: text: Full document text. Returns: List of text chunks, each <= chunk_size characters (approximately). You are the Backend stream agent for the Raven project. Work exclusively in the git worktree at `/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/.claude/worktrees/stream-backend` on branch `feat/stream-backend-m2-crud`.

## Your mission
Implement M2 backend issues #29, #28, #24, #25, #26, #27, #32 in the order listed. Each issue gets its own squash PR to `ravencloak-org/Raven`.

## Your plan
Read the full implementation plan at:
`/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-backend-m2-crud.md`

Follow it task-by-task using TDD (write failing test → run → implement → run → pass → commit).

## Quality gates — MANDATORY before each PR
1. All checkboxes in the GitHub issue ticked
2. `go test -short ./...` passes
3. `go build ./...` succeeds
4. `golangci-lint run` clean (or minimal warnings)
5. Feature works locally
6. PR uses `Closes #N` in body, squash merge style

## Key constraints
- Work only inside `/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/.claude/worktrees/stream-backend`
- Never push directly to main
- One PR per issue — do not batch
- Read existing code before writing (especially `internal/middleware/auth.go` for context keys, `internal/config/config.go` for Config struct, `pkg/apierror/apierror.go` for error helpers)
- The repo is `ravencloak-org/Raven`
- All PRs must be squash-merged (note this in PR description)

<task-notification>
<task-id>a2fbb86e7f818a779</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_01VCGEtNmY5bHpyGzSMidoup</tool-use-id>
<output-file>REDACTED.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review three-stream design spec" completed</summary>
<result>I now have everything I need to deliver a complete, evidence-based review. Here is my assessment.

---

## Design Spec Review: `2026-03-28-three-stream-parallel-development-design.md`

Overall verdict: **NOT APPROVED — 4 issues must be fixed before agents start.**

---

### What is done well

The three-worktree isolation model is sound. Using contracts committed to `main` before agents start is the correct approach for decoupled parallel development. The squash-merge-per-issue cadence matches the project's established convention. The prerequisite PRs (#23, #30, #31) are confirmed closed and their work is on `main`. The existing `proto/ai_worker.proto` is a solid foundation for the AI stream contract.

---

### Issues

#### CRITICAL — Issue 1: The `contracts/` directory does not exist and is not scheduled for creation

**Section 3 of the spec states:** "Committed to `main` before agents start, under `contracts/`". But `contracts/` does not exist in the repo. There is no issue, no task, and no agent assigned to create it. The spec describes Stream 2 (frontend) building against `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` and Stream 3 (AI) building against `contracts/raven_worker.proto`, but neither file exists.

The proto file does exist at `/proto/ai_worker.proto`, but the spec points the AI stream at `contracts/raven_worker.proto` — a different path and a different filename.

**Required fix:** Before the three agents start, either (a) create a pre-flight task that commits both contract files to `main` and clearly assigns it to a human or a fourth agent, or (b) update the spec to reference the actual proto path (`proto/ai_worker.proto`) and create the `openapi-stub.yaml` in a named location. The spec must also specify who does this work and when it is considered done. Agents must not start until both files are present on `main`.

---

#### CRITICAL — Issue 2: Backend dependency ordering is wrong — RBAC (#28) and RLS (#29) are placed before the APIs they must protect

The spec sequences: #24 (Org CRUD) → #28 (RBAC) → #29 (RLS) → #25 (Workspace CRUD) → ...

Checking the actual issues:
- Issue #25 (Workspace CRUD) states in its acceptance criteria: "Workspace CRUD operates within org tenant boundary (RLS enforced)." It explicitly depends on RLS being active.
- Issue #27 (KB CRUD) has role checks ("Only workspace members with `member` role or above...") baked into its acceptance criteria, requiring RBAC middleware.
- Issues #28 and #29 themselves only depend on #23 (JWT), which is already merged.

The current ordering is actually defensible — you can implement #28 and #29 as independent middleware/migration tasks right after #24, and then #25/27 are written with RBAC and RLS already available. However, the real problem is a missing dependency the spec omits entirely:

Issue #29 (RLS) depends on **#5 (Database Migrations)**. Issue #24 (Org CRUD) also depends on **#5**. Both #5 and #6 are closed (M1 complete), so this is not a blocker today, but the spec's "Dependencies satisfied" section only lists #23/#30/#31. It should also explicitly confirm #5 (Database Migrations) and #6 (Keycloak Realm) as satisfied, because issues #24, #29 (depend on #5) and #26 (depends on #6) will fail if those are not verified closed. The spec says "No blockers remain" but does not account for these two M1 dependencies. They are closed, but the spec is misleading in its omission.

**Required fix:** Add #5 and #6 to the "Dependencies satisfied" list in Section 2. This prevents any future confusion when an agent reads the spec and does not realize those prior issues must be closed.

---

#### CRITICAL — Issue 3: AI Stream dependency on issue #15 (Crawl4AI / Task 3.6) is silently dropped

Issue #16 (Text Chunking) states in its GitHub body: "Deps: Task 3.5, **3.6**." Task 3.6 is issue #15 (Crawl4AI Web Scraping), which is open, P1-important, and assigned to M3.

The spec's AI stream sequence is: #14 → #16 → #17. It skips #15 entirely. Issue #16 therefore has an unsatisfied declared dependency (#15) that the spec does not acknowledge, resolve, or explain away.

There are two valid resolutions: either insert #15 between #14 and #16 in the AI stream, or document a conscious decision that #15's Crawl4AI output is not needed for the chunker path exercised in this plan (e.g., the chunker will only operate on LiteParse-parsed content in M3 scope, and web scraping output is deferred). Neither resolution is currently in the spec.

**Required fix:** Either add #15 to the AI stream table between #14 and #16, or add an explicit note explaining why #15 is excluded and confirming that #16's test suite will not require Crawl4AI output.

---

#### CRITICAL — Issue 4: Issue #26 (User Management) has an unresolved external dependency on Keycloak SPI that is not addressed in this plan

Issue #26 depends on the Keycloak webhook endpoint (`POST /api/v1/internal/keycloak-webhook`) and the reavencloak SPI sending events. Its acceptance criteria explicitly require: "Keycloak webhook creates/updates user records." The SPI itself is issue #6, which is closed. However, the webhook path in issue #26 is new application code that creates a tight runtime coupling: it requires a live Keycloak instance configured to call the webhook.

The spec's quality gate #3 requires `docker compose up` and end-to-end validation. For issue #26, this means the `docker compose` environment must have Keycloak running with the SPI JAR loaded and pointed at the API. The spec does not mention this and gives no guidance on whether the docker compose environment is already configured for this. If it is not, issue #26 will fail its own acceptance criteria during the quality gate.

**Required fix:** Add a note under Stream 1 (or Section 4 quality gates) explicitly stating that for issue #26, the docker compose environment includes a working Keycloak instance with the reavencloak SPI, and that the internal webhook is testable locally. If the environment is not ready, #26 must be descoped to stub/mock the webhook and a follow-up issue created.

---

### Important (should fix, but not blockers to starting)

#### Issue 5: Issue #32 (Swagger) depends on #26 and #27 but the spec omits #26 from its declared dependencies

Issue #32's GitHub body lists: "Depends on #24, #25, #27." It does not list #26 (User Management), which introduces the `/me` endpoint and the internal webhook endpoint — both of which would be absent from the generated spec if #26 is not done first. Since the spec orders #32 last, this works out in practice, but the issue itself has an undeclared dependency. This is a data quality issue in the GitHub issue, not in the spec. Flag it for correction on the issue itself.

#### Issue 6: The "reconcile against real backend endpoints" step in Section 6 has no owner, no trigger, and no timeline

Section 6 states: "Frontend and AI streams reconcile against real backend endpoints once backend PRs land." This is an integration step that requires coordination across all three agents. There is no defined trigger (e.g., "when all Stream 1 PRs are merged"), no owner (which agent does the reconciliation?), and no quality gate for it. In a parallel multi-agent setup this is a coordination gap — it can result in an integration phase that never formally starts.

**Recommended fix:** Add a Section 7 "Integration Handoff" that defines: the trigger condition (all Stream 1 PRs squash-merged to `main`), who performs the reconciliation (frontend and AI agents each rebase their branches against `main`), and what the acceptance gate is (docker compose integration test with all three services).

#### Issue 7: The quality gate for the frontend stream references Playwright E2E but there is no running backend for those tests to target

The spec requires Playwright E2E tests as a gate before any push. But Stream 2 is developing against the OpenAPI stub mock, not a real backend. Playwright E2E tests for auth flows (OIDC PKCE, token refresh) require a live Keycloak and a real API. This either means the E2E gate cannot be satisfied in isolation, or the agent must run a mock server. The spec does not define what the Playwright E2E tests run against.

**Recommended fix:** Clarify in Section 4 that for the frontend stream, Playwright E2E tests run against a mock API server (e.g., `msw` or `openapi-backend` serving the stub YAML), not the real backend. Specify this explicitly so the agent does not block waiting for a live API.

---

### Suggestions (nice to have)

- Section 5 states agents "do not start the next issue until the current one has a passing PR." This is correct but ambiguous about what "passing" means — does it require CI green on GitHub, or just local tests? Given the repo has CI enforced on PRs, clarify that "passing PR" means GitHub Actions CI passes, not just local `go test`.

- The spec header says `Status: Approved` but this document is under review. That field should be `Status: Draft` until this review is resolved. Having `Approved` in the header of a document with open critical issues will confuse agents that read it as authoritative.

---

### Summary of required actions before agents start

| # | Action | Owner |
|---|--------|-------|
| 1 | Create `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` and place proto stub at the path the spec references; commit both to `main` | Human / pre-flight task |
| 2 | Add #5 and #6 to the "Dependencies satisfied" list in Section 2 | Spec author |
| 3 | Either add #15 to the AI stream or document explicitly why it is excluded | Spec author |
| 4 | Confirm or add docker compose Keycloak readiness note for #26's quality gate | Spec author |
| 5 | Add Section 7 "Integration Handoff" with trigger, owner, and acceptance gate | Spec author |
| 6 | Clarify Playwright E2E target (mock server vs. real API) in Section 4 | Spec author |
| 7 | Change header `Status` from `Approved` to `Draft` until all above are resolved | Spec author |</result>

</task-notification>

input.content:
openapi: "3.1.0"
info:
  title: Raven API (stub for frontend development)
  description: |
    Stub contract used by the frontend stream during parallel development.
    Reflects the real API shape that the backend stream will implement.
    Replace with real OpenAPI spec once backend PRs merge.
  version: "1.0.0"

servers:
  - url: /api/v1
    description: Local dev

security:
  - bearerAuth: []

components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT

  schemas:
    Organization:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:        { type: string, format: uuid }
        name:      { type: string }
        slug:      { type: string }
        status:    { type: string, enum: [active, deactivated] }
        settings:  { type: object }
        created_at: { type: string, format: date-time }
        updated_at: { type: string, format: date-time }
      required: [id, name, slug, status, created_at, updated_at]

    CreateOrgRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        name: { type: string, minLength: 2, maxLength: 255 }
      required: [name]

    UpdateOrgRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        name:     { type: string, minLength: 2, maxLength: 255 }
        settings: { type: object }

    OrgMember:
      type: object
      properties:
        user_id:  { type: string, format: uuid }
        email:    { type: string, format: email }
        org_role: { type: string, enum: [org_admin, member] }
        joined_at: { type: string, format: date-time }
      required: [user_id, email, org_role]

    Workspace:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:        { type: string, format: uuid }
        org_id:    { type: string, format: uuid }
        name:      { type: string }
        slug:      { type: string }
        settings:  { type: object }

      required: [id, org_id, name, slug, created_at, updated_at]

      type: object
      properties:
        name: { type: string, minLength: 2, maxLength: 255 }
      required: [name]

    WorkspaceMember:
      type: object
      properties:
        user_id:        { type: string, format: uuid }
        email:          { type: string, format: email }
        workspace_role: { type: string, enum: [owner, admin, member, viewer] }
      required: [user_id, email, workspace_role]

    KnowledgeBase:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:           { type: string, format: uuid }
        org_id:       { type: string, format: uuid }
        workspace_id: { type: string, format: uuid }

        settings:     { type: object }

      required: [id, email, org_id, org_role]

    ErrorResponse:
      type: object

        message: { type: string }

      required: [code, message]

    PaginatedList:
      type: object
      properties:
        items:  { type: array, items: {} }
        total:  { type: integer }
        offset: { type: integer }
        limit:  { type: integer }
      required: [items, total]

paths:
  /orgs:
    post:
      summary: Create organization
      tags: [Organizations]
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/CreateOrgRequest' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created

            application/json:

          description: Validation error
          content:

              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse' }

  /orgs/{org_id}:
    get:

      tags: [Organizations]

          in: path

        '200':
          description: OK
          content:

          description: Not found

              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse' }

      responses:

  /orgs/{org_id}/members:
    get:

      parameters:

          in: path

          content:

  /orgs/{org_id}/workspaces:
    get:

      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
        - name: offset
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, default: 0 }
        - name: limit
          in: query
          schema: { type: integer, default: 20, maximum: 100 }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList' }
    post:
      summary: Create workspace
      tags: [Workspaces]
      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/CreateWorkspaceRequest' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Workspace' }

  /orgs/{org_id}/workspaces/{ws_id}:
    get:
      summary: Get workspace
      tags: [Workspaces]
      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
        - name: ws_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Workspace' }
    delete:
      summary: Soft-delete workspace
      tags: [Workspaces]
      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
        - name: ws_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
      responses:
        '204':
          description: Deleted

  /orgs/{org_id}/workspaces/{ws_id}/members:
    post:
      summary: Add workspace member
      tags: [Workspaces]
      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
        - name: ws_id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema: { type: string, format: uuid }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                user_id: { type: string, format: uuid }
                role:    { type: string, enum: [owner, admin, member, viewer] }
              required: [user_id, role]
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Added

  /orgs/{org_id}/workspaces/{ws_id}/knowledge-bases:
    get:
      summary: List knowledge bases
      tags: [KnowledgeBases]
      parameters:
        - name: org_id
          in: path
          required: true

        - name: ws_id
          in: path
          required: true

      responses:

    post:

              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/KnowledgeBase' }

              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/User' }

content.text: Now update the design spec to fix all four critical issues.

content.content:
1	# Three-Stream Parallel Development — Design Spec
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-03-28
4	**Status:** Approved
5	**Milestone scope:** M2 (backend CRUD + security), M5 (frontend scaffold + auth), M3 (AI worker ingestion)
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Stream Architecture
10	
11	Three isolated git worktrees, each owned by one parallel agent running concurrently:
12	
13	```
14	Raven/
15	├── (main — integration target)
16	└── .claude/worktrees/
17	    ├── stream-backend/    ← feat/stream-backend-m2-crud
18	    ├── stream-frontend/   ← feat/stream-frontend-m5-scaffold
19	    └── stream-ai/         ← feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion
20	```
21	
22	All open M2 PRs (#100 JWT, #101 CORS, #103 rate limiting, #104 CI fix) are **merged**. No blockers remain.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. Scope per Stream
27	
28	### Stream 1 — Backend (M2: Core API + Auth)
29	
30	Sequential within one worktree. Each issue gets its own squash PR after full local validation.
31	
32	| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
33	|-------|-------|-------|----------|
34	| 1 | #24 | Organization CRUD API | P0-blocker |
35	| 2 | #28 | Role-Based Access Control Middleware | P0-blocker |
36	| 3 | #29 | PostgreSQL RLS Policies | P0-blocker |
37	| 4 | #25 | Workspace CRUD API | P0-blocker |
38	| 5 | #26 | User Management API (Keycloak Sync) | P0-blocker |
39	| 6 | #27 | Knowledge Base CRUD API | P0-blocker |
40	| 7 | #32 | Swagger/OpenAPI Generation | P2-nice-to-have |
41	
42	**Dependencies satisfied:** #23 (JWT), #30 (CORS/versioning), #31 (rate limiting) all merged into main.
43	
44	### Stream 2 — Frontend (M5: Admin Dashboard)
45	
46	| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
47	|-------|-------|-------|----------|
48	| 1 | #41 | Vue.js App Scaffold with Tailwind Plus | P0-blocker |
49	| 2 | #42 | Auth Flow (Keycloak OIDC PKCE) | P0-blocker |
50	| 3 | #43 | Organization Management Pages | P0-blocker |
51	
52	Builds against `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` until backend PRs merge.
53	
54	### Stream 3 — AI Worker (M3: Ingestion Pipeline)
55	
56	| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
57	|-------|-------|-------|----------|
58	| 1 | #14 | Python Worker: LiteParse Integration | P0-blocker |
59	| 2 | #16 | Python Worker: Text Chunking | P0-blocker |
60	| 3 | #17 | Python Worker: Multi-Provider Embedding (BYOK) | P0-blocker |
61	
62	Builds against `contracts/raven_worker.proto` stub.
63	
64	---
65	
66	## 3. Interface Contracts
67	
68	Committed to `main` before agents start, under `contracts/`:
69	
70	- **`contracts/openapi-stub.yaml`** — REST stubs for Org, Workspace, KB, User endpoints (paths, request/response schemas, Bearer JWT auth). Frontend develops against this mock.
71	- **`contracts/raven_worker.proto`** — gRPC service stubs: `DocumentIngest`, `ChunkText`, `GenerateEmbedding`. AI worker implements these interfaces.
72	
73	---
74	
75	## 4. Quality Gates (all streams, no exceptions)
76	
77	Before any `git push` or PR creation:
78	
79	1. **All issue task checkboxes** satisfied (every `- [ ]` in the GitHub issue ticked)
80	2. **Tests pass locally:**
81	   - Backend: `go test ./...` (unit + integration)
82	   - Frontend: `vitest` (unit) + Playwright (E2E)
83	   - AI Worker: `pytest` (unit + integration)
84	3. **End-to-end validation:** `docker compose up` — feature works as intended locally
85	4. **PR format:** squash merge, linked to its issue with `Closes #N`
86	
87	---
88	
89	## 5. Agent Responsibilities
90	
91	Each agent:
92	- Works only within its own worktree branch — never touches main directly
93	- Creates one PR per issue (not batched)
94	- Runs all tests locally before pushing
95	- Follows existing code patterns in the repo (reads codebase before writing)
96	- Does not start the next issue until the current one has a passing PR
97	
98	---
99	
100	## 6. Integration Strategy
101	
102	- Each stream's PRs merge into `main` via squash merge as they complete
103	- Frontend and AI streams reconcile against real backend endpoints once backend PRs land
104	- Contract stubs are replaced by real implementations as backend PRs merge
105	- No cross-stream code sharing within worktrees — integration happens only through `main`
106	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Three-Stream Parallel Development — Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-03-28
**Status:** Approved
**Milestone scope:** M2 (backend CRUD + security), M5 (frontend scaffold + auth), M3 (AI worker ingestion)

---

## 1. Stream Architecture

Three isolated git worktrees, each owned by one parallel agent running concurrently:

```
Raven/
├── (main — integration target)
└── .claude/worktrees/
    ├── stream-backend/    ← feat/stream-backend-m2-crud
    ├── stream-frontend/   ← feat/stream-frontend-m5-scaffold
    └── stream-ai/         ← feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion
```

All open M2 PRs (#100 JWT, #101 CORS, #103 rate limiting, #104 CI fix) are **merged**. No blockers remain.

---

## 2. Scope per Stream

### Stream 1 — Backend (M2: Core API + Auth)

Sequential within one worktree. Each issue gets its own squash PR after full local validation.

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #24 | Organization CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #28 | Role-Based Access Control Middleware | P0-blocker |
| 3 | #29 | PostgreSQL RLS Policies | P0-blocker |
| 4 | #25 | Workspace CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 5 | #26 | User Management API (Keycloak Sync) | P0-blocker |
| 6 | #27 | Knowledge Base CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 7 | #32 | Swagger/OpenAPI Generation | P2-nice-to-have |

**Dependencies satisfied:** #23 (JWT), #30 (CORS/versioning), #31 (rate limiting) all merged into main.

### Stream 2 — Frontend (M5: Admin Dashboard)

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #41 | Vue.js App Scaffold with Tailwind Plus | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #42 | Auth Flow (Keycloak OIDC PKCE) | P0-blocker |
| 3 | #43 | Organization Management Pages | P0-blocker |

Builds against `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` until backend PRs merge.

### Stream 3 — AI Worker (M3: Ingestion Pipeline)

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #14 | Python Worker: LiteParse Integration | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #16 | Python Worker: Text Chunking | P0-blocker |
| 3 | #17 | Python Worker: Multi-Provider Embedding (BYOK) | P0-blocker |

Builds against `contracts/raven_worker.proto` stub.

---

## 3. Interface Contracts

Committed to `main` before agents start, under `contracts/`:

- **`contracts/openapi-stub.yaml`** — REST stubs for Org, Workspace, KB, User endpoints (paths, request/response schemas, Bearer JWT auth). Frontend develops against this mock.
- **`contracts/raven_worker.proto`** — gRPC service stubs: `DocumentIngest`, `ChunkText`, `GenerateEmbedding`. AI worker implements these interfaces.

---

## 4. Quality Gates (all streams, no exceptions)

Before any `git push` or PR creation:

1. **All issue task checkboxes** satisfied (every `- [ ]` in the GitHub issue ticked)
2. **Tests pass locally:**
   - Backend: `go test ./...` (unit + integration)
   - Frontend: `vitest` (unit) + Playwright (E2E)
   - AI Worker: `pytest` (unit + integration)
3. **End-to-end validation:** `docker compose up` — feature works as intended locally
4. **PR format:** squash merge, linked to its issue with `Closes #N`

---

## 5. Agent Responsibilities

Each agent:
- Works only within its own worktree branch — never touches main directly
- Creates one PR per issue (not batched)
- Runs all tests locally before pushing
- Follows existing code patterns in the repo (reads codebase before writing)
- Does not start the next issue until the current one has a passing PR

---

## 6. Integration Strategy

- Each stream's PRs merge into `main` via squash merge as they complete
- Frontend and AI streams reconcile against real backend endpoints once backend PRs land
- Contract stubs are replaced by real implementations as backend PRs merge
- No cross-stream code sharing within worktrees — integration happens only through `main`

# Three-Stream Parallel Development — Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-03-28
**Status:** Active
**Milestone scope:** M2 (backend CRUD + security), M5 (frontend scaffold + auth), M3 (AI worker ingestion)

---

## 1. Stream Architecture

Three isolated git worktrees, each owned by one parallel agent running concurrently:

```
Raven/
├── (main — integration target)
└── .claude/worktrees/
    ├── stream-backend/    ← feat/stream-backend-m2-crud
    ├── stream-frontend/   ← feat/stream-frontend-m5-scaffold
    └── stream-ai/         ← feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion
```

All open M2 PRs (#100 JWT, #101 CORS, #103 rate limiting, #104 CI fix) are **merged**. No blockers remain.

---

## 2. Scope per Stream

### Stream 1 — Backend (M2: Core API + Auth)

Sequential within one worktree. Each issue gets its own squash PR after full local validation.

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #29 | PostgreSQL RLS Policies | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #28 | Role-Based Access Control Middleware | P0-blocker |
| 3 | #24 | Organization CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 4 | #25 | Workspace CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 5 | #26 | User Management API (Keycloak Sync) | P0-blocker |
| 6 | #27 | Knowledge Base CRUD API | P0-blocker |
| 7 | #32 | Swagger/OpenAPI Generation | P2-nice-to-have |

**Dependencies satisfied (all confirmed closed):**
- #23 (JWT middleware) — merged via PR #100
- #30 (CORS/versioning) — merged via PR #101
- #31 (rate limiting) — merged via PR #103
- #5 (database migrations / M1 DB setup) — closed, M1 complete 2026-03-27
- #6 (Keycloak realm setup) — closed, M1 complete 2026-03-27

**Note on #26 (User Management):** Issue #26 requires a Keycloak webhook. The `docker-compose.yml` in this repo includes Keycloak with the reavencloak SPI pre-loaded. The internal webhook endpoint (`POST /api/v1/internal/keycloak-webhook`) must be verified reachable only from the compose network (not externally). If Keycloak SPI is not yet emitting events in the local environment, the webhook handler can be implemented and unit-tested with mocked payloads — a follow-up integration test can be added once the SPI is configured. Do not block the PR on a live Keycloak webhook firing.

### Stream 2 — Frontend (M5: Admin Dashboard)

> **Note:** #41 (Vue scaffold) is already implemented — the `frontend/` directory has a complete Vue 3 + TypeScript + Tailwind scaffold. Start directly at #42.

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #42 | Auth Flow (Keycloak OIDC PKCE) | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #43 | Organization Management Pages | P0-blocker |

Builds against `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` (committed to `main`). Playwright E2E tests use `page.route()` to mock API responses — no live backend required during development.

### Stream 3 — AI Worker (M3: Ingestion Pipeline)

> **Note on #15 (Crawl4AI):** Issue #16 declares a dependency on #15 (Crawl4AI web scraping). This is a runtime pipeline dependency — the chunker processes text regardless of its source. The `TextChunker` implementation in #16 has no code dependency on #15. #15 is therefore excluded from this plan; it will be implemented separately. The AI stream's test suite mocks document content rather than calling the scraper.

| Order | Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|-------|----------|
| 1 | #14 | Python Worker: LiteParse Integration | P0-blocker |
| 2 | #16 | Python Worker: Text Chunking | P0-blocker |
| 3 | #17 | Python Worker: Multi-Provider Embedding (BYOK) | P0-blocker |

Builds against `proto/ai_worker.proto` (already exists in the repo). The proto file is the authoritative contract — do not modify it.

---

## 3. Interface Contracts

Both contracts committed to `main` and available at launch:

- **`contracts/openapi-stub.yaml`** — REST stubs for Org, Workspace, KB, User endpoints (paths, request/response schemas, Bearer JWT auth). Frontend mocks API calls against this during development.
- **`proto/ai_worker.proto`** — gRPC service definition: `ParseAndEmbed`, `QueryRAG`, `GetEmbedding`. Already exists. AI worker implements these interfaces. Do not modify during M3 work.

---

## 4. Quality Gates (all streams, no exceptions)

Before any `git push` or PR creation:

1. **All issue task checkboxes** satisfied (every `- [ ]` in the GitHub issue ticked)
2. **Tests pass locally:**
   - Backend: `go test -short ./...` (unit); integration tests require `docker compose up`
   - Frontend: `vitest` (unit) + Playwright E2E with `page.route()` mocks (no live backend needed)
   - AI Worker: `pytest -v` (unit + mocked integration)
3. **Build succeeds:**
   - Backend: `go build ./...` + `golangci-lint run`
   - Frontend: `npm run build` (TypeScript + Vite, zero errors)
   - AI Worker: `ruff check .` + `ruff format --check .`
4. **PR format:** squash merge, linked to its issue with `Closes #N`
5. **"Passing PR"** means GitHub Actions CI passes on the PR — not just local tests.

---

## 5. Agent Responsibilities

Each agent:
- Works only within its own worktree branch — never touches main directly
- Creates one PR per issue (not batched)
- Runs all tests locally before pushing
- Follows existing code patterns in the repo (reads codebase before writing)
- Does not start the next issue until the current PR has CI passing on GitHub

---

## 6. Integration Strategy

- Each stream's PRs merge into `main` via squash merge as they complete
- Frontend and AI streams reconcile against real backend endpoints once backend PRs land
- Contract stubs are replaced by real implementations as backend PRs merge
- No cross-stream code sharing within worktrees — integration happens only through `main`

---

## 7. Integration Handoff

**Trigger:** All Stream 1 (backend) PRs are squash-merged to `main`.

**Actions:**
1. Stream 2 (frontend) agent rebases `feat/stream-frontend-m5-scaffold` on updated `main`, replaces `page.route()` mocks with real API calls to the local backend, re-runs full Playwright suite.
2. Stream 3 (AI) agent rebases `feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion` on updated `main`, runs an end-to-end gRPC call through the full `ParseAndEmbed` pipeline with a real document.

**Acceptance gate:** `docker compose up` with all three services — backend, frontend dev server, AI worker — produces correct responses for the full ingest → query flow. This gate is owned by the human (Jobin) after agents complete their streams.

<result>I have all the information I need from the plan and spec — the issue checklists are fully defined in the plan file. I also need Bash access and GitHub tool access to complete this task. Let me explain exactly what I need and why:

---

**Tools I need to complete this task:**

**1. Bash** — Essential for every step:
- `npm install keycloak-js @playwright/test vitest @vue/test-utils happy-dom` — add dependencies
- `npx vitest run src/stores/auth.spec.ts` — TDD red/green cycles (required by plan)
- `npm run build` — TypeScript quality gate before each PR
- `npm run lint` — lint quality gate before each PR
- `npx playwright install chromium` + `npx playwright test` — E2E tests
- `git add` + `git commit` + `git push origin feat/stream-frontend-m5-scaffold` — commit and push work

**2. `mcp__github__issue_read`** — To read the exact task checklists in issues #42 and #43, so I can tick every checkbox (quality gate #1 requires "all issue task checkboxes satisfied").

**3. `mcp__github__create_pull_request`** — To create one squash-ready PR per issue with `Closes #42` / `Closes #43` in the body, as required by the spec.

---

I can write all the source files — `auth.ts`, `auth.spec.ts`, `orgs.ts`, `orgs.spec.ts`, `OrgDetailPage.vue`, `OrgListPage.vue`, `playwright.config.ts`, `e2e/auth.spec.ts`, `e2e/orgs.spec.ts`, updated `router/index.ts`, `main.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `useAuth.ts`, `.env.example`, and `public/silent-check-sso.html` — since I've read all the existing files and the plan is clear.

However, I cannot run tests to verify correctness, cannot install dependencies that the TypeScript needs to compile, cannot commit, and cannot push or create PRs.

**Would you like me to:**
- **(A)** Write all the files now (everything I can do without Bash/GitHub), and you grant Bash + GitHub tool access afterward to run tests, commit, and create PRs?
- **(B)** Grant Bash access first so I can follow the TDD cycle properly (write failing test → run → implement → run passing → commit)?

Option B is the correct approach per the plan's TDD requirement and will produce the highest-quality output. Option A risks writing code that fails TypeScript compilation in ways I can't catch without running the build.</result>

<result>All files look correct. Now I need Bash to complete the remaining steps: install packages, run tests, lint, commit, and create PRs. Let me explain what was done and what still needs to happen.

---

Here is a summary of everything completed and what still needs to happen:

## What was done (all code written)

All implementation files have been written in `/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/.claude/worktrees/stream-ai/ai-worker/` on branch `feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion`:

**Task 1 — Dependencies (`pyproject.toml`)**
Added `liteparse>=0.3.0`, `langchain-text-splitters>=0.3.0`, `openai>=1.58.0`, `tiktoken>=0.8.0`, `httpx>=0.28.0` to `[project] dependencies`.

**Task 2 — Issue #14 LiteParse (`processors/parser.py` + `tests/test_parser.py`)**
- `DocumentParser.parse()` is async, runs liteparse in a thread pool via `run_in_executor` (non-blocking).
- MIME resolution with extension fallback for `.pdf`, `.docx`, `.pptx`, `.html`, `.md`, `.txt`.
- Raises `ValueError("Unsupported...")` for unknown MIME types — matching the test assertion.
- Returns `""` for empty content immediately (before hitting liteparse).

**Task 3 — Issue #16 Chunking (`processors/chunker.py` + `tests/test_chunker.py`)**
- Replaced the basic sliding-window stub with `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` from `langchain-text-splitters`.
- Separators: `["\n\n", "\n", ". ", "! ", "? ", " ", ""]` — splits on natural boundaries first.

**Task 4 — Issue #17 Embedding (`providers/base.py`, `providers/openai_provider.py`, `services/embedding.py`, `config.py`, `server.py`, `tests/test_embedding.py`, `tests/conftest.py`)**
- `EmbeddingProvider` protocol updated: `runtime_checkable`, `embed(text, model=None)` signature.
- `OpenAIEmbeddingProvider` wraps `openai.AsyncOpenAI`, supports per-call model override.
- `EmbeddingServicer` now takes a `provider` constructor argument (dependency injection).
- `config.py` gained `openai_api_key: str = ""` and `embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-3-small"`.
- `server.py` wires `OpenAIEmbeddingProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key, model=settings.embedding_model)` and passes it to `EmbeddingServicer`. `GetEmbedding` now calls `self._embedding.GetEmbedding(...)` (was `get_embedding`).
- `conftest.py` gained a `grpc_context` fixture (AsyncMock with `.abort`).

## What still needs Bash permission to complete

The following steps are blocked by the Bash denial and must be run manually or after permission is granted:

```bash
# From: /Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/.claude/worktrees/stream-ai/ai-worker

# 1. Install dependencies
python -m pip install -e ".[dev]"

# 2. Verify liteparse API (may need to adjust parser.py if API differs)
python -c "import liteparse; help(liteparse)"

# 3. Run tests
python -m pytest -v

# 4. Lint check
python -m ruff check . && python -m ruff format --check .

# 5. Three commits (from worktree root: /Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/.claude/worktrees/stream-ai)
git add ai-worker/pyproject.toml
git commit -m "deps(ai-worker): add liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai, tiktoken"

git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/parser.py ai-worker/tests/test_parser.py
git commit -m "feat(#14): implement LiteParse document parsing (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, MD, TXT)"

git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py
git commit -m "feat(#16): semantic text chunking with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter"

git add ai-worker/
git commit -m "feat(#17): multi-provider embedding (OpenAI BYOK) with EmbeddingProvider protocol"

# 6. Push and create PRs
git push origin feat/stream-ai-m3-ingestion
gh pr create --title "feat: LiteParse document parsing (#14)" --body "Closes #14" --repo ravencloak-org/Raven
gh pr create --title "feat: semantic text chunking (#16)" --body "Closes #16" --repo ravencloak-org/Raven
gh pr create --title "feat: multi-provider embedding BYOK (#17)" --body "Closes #17" --repo ravencloak-org/Raven
```

**One risk to watch for:** the liteparse `Document` constructor API — the plan specifies `liteparse.Document(content=io.BytesIO(...), mime_type=..., filename=...)`. If the installed version uses different parameter names (e.g., `file_path` instead of `content`), `parser.py`'s `_parse_sync` method will need adjustment. Run `python -c "import liteparse; help(liteparse.Document)"` to confirm before running the tests.</result>

**Task 2 — Issue #14 LiteParse (`processors/parser.py` + `tests/test_parser.py`)**
- `DocumentParser.parse()` is async, runs liteparse in a thread pool via `run_in_executor` (non-blocking).
- MIME resolution with extension fallback for `.pdf`, `.docx`, `.pptx`, `.html`, `.md`, `.txt`.
- Raises `ValueError("Unsupported...")` for unknown MIME types — matching the test assertion.

**Task 3 — Issue #16 Chunking (`processors/chunker.py` + `tests/test_chunker.py`)**
- Replaced the basic sliding-window stub with `RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter` from `langchain-text-splitters`.
- Separators: `["\n\n", "\n", ". ", "! ", "? ", " ", ""]` — splits on natural boundaries first.

**Task 4 — Issue #17 Embedding (`providers/base.py`, `providers/openai_provider.py`, `services/embedding.py`, `config.py`, `server.py`, `tests/test_embedding.py`, `tests/conftest.py`)**
- `EmbeddingProvider` protocol updated: `runtime_checkable`, `embed(text, model=None)` signature.
- `OpenAIEmbeddingProvider` wraps `openai.AsyncOpenAI`, supports per-call model override.
- `EmbeddingServicer` now takes a `provider` constructor argument (dependency injection).
- `config.py` gained `openai_api_key: str = ""` and `embedding_model: str = "text-embedding-3-small"`.
- `server.py` wires `OpenAIEmbeddingProvider(api_key=settings.openai_api_key, model=settings.embedding_model)` and passes it to `EmbeddingServicer`. `GetEmbedding` now calls `self._embedding.GetEmbedding(...)` (was `get_embedding`).
- `conftest.py` gained a `grpc_context` fixture (AsyncMock with `.abort`).

```bash

## Step 4 — Commit in three separate commits (from worktree root)

git commit -m "deps(ai-worker): add liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai, tiktoken"

git add ai-worker/raven_worker/processors/chunker.py ai-worker/tests/test_chunker.py
git commit -m "feat(#16): semantic text chunking with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter"

git add ai-worker/

## Step 5 — Push and create PRs (one per issue)

- MIME resolution with file extension fallback

Closes #14
EOF
)"

  --title "feat: semantic text chunking (#16)" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Splits on paragraph → sentence → word → character boundaries
- Configurable chunk_size and chunk_overlap

Closes #16
EOF
)"

  --title "feat: multi-provider embedding BYOK (#17)" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Defines EmbeddingProvider protocol (runtime_checkable)
- Implements OpenAIEmbeddingProvider with async openai SDK
- EmbeddingServicer now accepts provider via dependency injection
- Config gains openai_api_key and embedding_model fields
- Server wires provider from config

Closes #17
EOF
)"
```

## Constraints
- One PR per issue — do not batch into one PR

`/Users/jobinlawrance/Project/raven/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-frontend-m5-scaffold.md`

- One PR per issue, never batch

13	// OrgServicer is the interface the handler requires from the service layer.
14	type OrgServicer interface {
15		Create(ctx context.Context, req model.CreateOrgRequest) (*model.Organization, error)
16		GetByID(ctx context.Context, orgID string) (*model.Organization, error)
17		Update(ctx context.Context, orgID string, req model.UpdateOrgRequest) (*model.Organization, error)
18		Delete(ctx context.Context, orgID string) error
19	}
20	
21	// OrgHandler handles HTTP requests for organisation management.
22	type OrgHandler struct {
23		svc OrgServicer
24	}
25	
26	// NewOrgHandler creates a new OrgHandler.
27	func NewOrgHandler(svc OrgServicer) *OrgHandler {
28		return &OrgHandler{svc: svc}
29	}
30	
31	// Create handles POST /api/v1/orgs.
32	// Only org_admin may create organisations; enforced by RequireOrgRole at the route level.
33	func (h *OrgHandler) Create(c *gin.Context) {
34		var req model.CreateOrgRequest
35		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
36			appErr := &apierror.AppError{
37				Code:    http.StatusUnprocessableEntity,
38				Message: "Unprocessable Entity",
39				Detail:  err.Error(),
40			}
41			_ = c.Error(appErr)
42			c.Abort()
43			return
44		}
45		org, err := h.svc.Create(c.Request.Context(), req)
46		if err != nil {
47			_ = c.Error(err)
48			c.Abort()
49			return
50		}
51		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, org)
52	}
53	
54	// Get handles GET /api/v1/orgs/:org_id.
55	func (h *OrgHandler) Get(c *gin.Context) {
56		orgID := c.Param("org_id")
57		org, err := h.svc.GetByID(c.Request.Context(), orgID)
58		if err != nil {
59			_ = c.Error(err)
60			c.Abort()
61			return
62		}
63		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, org)
64	}
65	
66	// Update handles PUT /api/v1/orgs/:org_id.
67	func (h *OrgHandler) Update(c *gin.Context) {
68		orgID := c.Param("org_id")
69		// Enforce the caller's org matches the target org (unless org_admin).
70		callerOrgID, _ := c.Get(string(middleware.ContextKeyOrgID))
71		callerRole, _ := c.Get(string(middleware.ContextKeyOrgRole))
72		if callerRole != "org_admin" && callerOrgID != orgID {

77		}
78	
79		var req model.UpdateOrgRequest

84				Detail:  err.Error(),
85			}
86			_ = c.Error(appErr)

88			return

91		if err != nil {
92			_ = c.Error(err)
93			c.Abort()
94			return
95		}
96		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, org)
97	}

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested: (1) look at M2 milestone issues, (2) pick 2-4 features for parallel work, (3) create git worktrees for each, and (4) launch parallel agents using superpowers and context MCP. Additional critical constraints added mid-conversation: write and run tests locally before any push; Playwright tests for frontend; all GitHub issue task checkboxes must be ticked before creating PRs; don't touch issues already in open PRs or dependent on unmerged PRs; use squash merge for all PRs.

   The user then confirmed all pending M2 PRs (#100 JWT, #101 CORS, #103 rate limiting, #104 CI fix) had been merged, unblocking all M2 issues. They approved a 3-stream parallel approach: Backend (M2), Frontend (M5), AI Worker (M3). They also asked about 3 separate "cloud sessions" for backend/frontend/AI, which informed the 3-stream design.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Git worktrees for parallel isolated development (`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend`, `stream-frontend`, `stream-ai`)
   - Go (Gin, pgx/v5, goose migrations) for backend
   - Vue 3 + TypeScript + Tailwind + Pinia + vue-router for frontend
   - Python gRPC server (liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai SDK) for AI worker
   - Keycloak OIDC PKCE authentication
   - PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS) with `SET LOCAL app.current_org_id`
   - RBAC middleware with 4-layer access model (org_admin > owner > admin > member > viewer)
   - TDD: write failing test → run → implement → pass → commit
   - OpenAPI contract-first development (`contracts/openapi-stub.yaml`)
   - Squash merge per issue, one PR per issue
   - Subagent-driven development with bypassPermissions mode
   - pgx/v5 with raw SQL (no ORM/sqlc)
   - `c.AbortWithStatusJSON()` pattern (NOT `c.Error()+c.Abort()`) for handler error responses
   - Playwright with `page.route()` mocks for frontend E2E (no live backend needed)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-28-three-stream-parallel-development-design.md`**
     - Design spec for 3-stream parallel development. Updated after spec review to fix: add `contracts/openapi-stub.yaml` (created), fix proto path to `proto/ai_worker.proto`, add #5/#6 to satisfied dependencies, document #15 Crawl4AI exclusion, add #26 Keycloak webhook guidance, add Section 7 Integration Handoff, clarify Playwright uses page.route() mocks, change status from "Approved" to "Active".

   - **`contracts/openapi-stub.yaml`**
     - Created: Full OpenAPI 3.1.0 stub covering Org, Workspace, KB, User endpoints. Schemas include Organization, Workspace, KnowledgeBase, User, OrgMember, WorkspaceMember, CreateOrgRequest, ErrorResponse, PaginatedList. All paths under `/api/v1`. Used by frontend stream for page.route() mocking.

   - **`.gitignore`**
     - Added entries: `.worktrees/` and `.claude/worktrees/` to prevent worktree content from being tracked.

   - **`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-backend-m2-crud.md`**
     - Full TDD implementation plan for backend: Task 1 (pgx foundation + db.go), Task 2 (#29 RLS migration), Task 3 (#28 RBAC), Task 4 (#24 Org CRUD), Task 5 (#25 Workspace CRUD), Task 6 (#26 User Mgmt), Task 7 (#27 KB CRUD), Task 8 (#32 Swagger).

   - **`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-frontend-m5-scaffold.md`**
     - Full TDD plan for frontend: Task 1 (keycloak-js + auth store replacement for #42), Task 2 (Playwright setup), Task 3 (#43 Org pages with Pinia store + page.route() mocks).

   - **`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-28-ai-m3-ingestion.md`**
     - Full TDD plan for AI worker: Task 1 (add deps), Task 2 (#14 LiteParse), Task 3 (#16 chunking), Task 4 (#17 embedding).

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/db/db.go`**
     - Created by agent. Fixed: changed `pgxpool.Tx` → `pgx.Tx` in `WithOrgID` signature. Key functions: `New()` (pool init), `SetOrgIDQuery()`, `WithOrgID()` (RLS transaction helper).
     ```go
     func WithOrgID(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, orgID string, fn func(tx pgx.Tx) error) error
     ```

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/middleware/rbac.go`**
     - Fixed: replaced `c.Error(appErr) + c.Abort()` with `c.AbortWithStatusJSON(403, ...)` so 403 is written directly without needing ErrorHandler middleware in tests.
     ```go
     func RequireOrgRole(required string) gin.HandlerFunc {
         return func(c *gin.Context) {
             role, _ := c.Get(string(ContextKeyOrgRole))
             roleStr, _ := role.(string)
             if roleStr == "org_admin" || roleStr == required {
                 c.Next()
                 return
             }
             c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusForbidden, apierror.AppError{
                 Code: 403, Message: "Forbidden", Detail: "requires org role: " + required,
             })
         }
     }
     ```

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/handler/org.go`**
     - Fixed: replaced `c.Error(422 AppError) + c.Abort()` with `c.AbortWithStatusJSON(422, ...)` for validation failures. Tests were getting 400 instead of 422 because the error handler middleware wasn't in the test router.
     ```go
     if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
         c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, apierror.AppError{
             Code: 422, Message: "Unprocessable Entity", Detail: err.Error(),
         })
         return
     }
     ```

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/migrations/00015_rls_policies.sql`**
     - Created by agent. Verification migration for #29 — most RLS already exists in migrations 00004-00014. Adds RLS to any remaining tables not already covered.

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/handler/workspace.go`, `workspace_test.go`**
     - Written by agent (untracked, not yet staged/committed).

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/handler/user.go`**, `model/user.go`, `repository/user.go`, `service/user.go`
     - Written by agent (untracked, not yet staged/committed).

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-backend/internal/model/workspace.go`**, `repository/workspace.go`, `service/workspace.go`
     - Written by agent (untracked, not yet staged/committed).

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-ai/ai-worker/` (multiple files modified)**
     - `pyproject.toml`: added liteparse, langchain-text-splitters, openai, tiktoken, httpx
     - `processors/parser.py`: LiteParse implementation (async, thread pool, MIME resolution)
     - `processors/chunker.py`: RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter impl
     - `providers/base.py`: EmbeddingProvider protocol (runtime_checkable)
     - `providers/openai_provider.py`: OpenAI async embedding
     - `services/embedding.py`: dependency injection pattern
     - `config.py`: added openai_api_key, embedding_model fields
     - `server.py`: wired OpenAIEmbeddingProvider from config
     - `tests/conftest.py`: added grpc_context fixture
     - New test files: `test_parser.py`, `test_chunker.py`, `test_embedding.py`
     - NOTE: `pip install -e ".[dev]"` NOT YET RUN; liteparse API not yet verified

   - **`.claude/worktrees/stream-frontend/frontend/` (multiple files modified)**
     - `stores/auth.ts`: Replaced email/password stub with full Keycloak OIDC PKCE store
     - `main.ts`: Added `authStore.init()` before app mount
     - `router/index.ts`: Added auth guard with `router.beforeEach`
     - `vite.config.ts`: Added vitest test config (happy-dom environment)
     - `package.json`: Added keycloak-js, @playwright/test, vitest, @vue/test-utils
     - New files: `stores/auth.spec.ts`, `stores/orgs.ts`, `stores/orgs.spec.ts`, `api/orgs.ts`, `pages/orgs/OrgDetailPage.vue`, `playwright.config.ts`, `e2e/auth.spec.ts`, `e2e/orgs.spec.ts`, `.env.example`, `public/silent-check-sso.html`
     - NOTE: `npm install` was run (package-lock.json modified) but tests NOT yet run

4. Errors and Fixes:

   - **pgx/v5 not in go.mod**: Agents wrote Go files importing pgx before running `go get`. Fixed by running `go get github.com/jackc/pgx/v5@latest && go mod tidy` directly in the main session from the stream-backend worktree.

   - **`db.go` type error `pgxpool.Tx` vs `pgx.Tx`**: `pgxpool.Pool.Begin()` returns `pgx.Tx` (not `pgxpool.Tx` — that type doesn't exist). Fixed by adding `"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5"` import and changing function signature to `fn func(tx pgx.Tx) error`.

   - **RBAC middleware returning 200 instead of 403**: `c.Error(appErr) + c.Abort()` stores the error but doesn't write a response. Test routers in `rbac_test.go` use `gin.New()` without the `apierror.ErrorHandler()` middleware, so no response was written. Fixed by using `c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusForbidden, ...)` which writes the response directly.

   - **Org handler returning 400 instead of 422**: Same root cause — `c.Error(422 AppError) + c.Abort()` without ErrorHandler middleware. The AppError's Code field (422) was never used to set the HTTP status. Fixed by using `c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, ...)`.

   - **Subagents cannot access Bash tool**: Despite launching with `mode: "bypassPermissions"`, all subagents consistently report Bash is denied. This is a session-level permission issue. Three separate agent launches (without mode, then with bypassPermissions) all failed. Solution: main session handles all shell operations (go test, go build, git, npm, pip, etc.) directly.

   - **Spec reviewer found critical issues before agents started**: (1) contracts/ didn't exist, (2) #5/#6 not listed as satisfied, (3) #15 dependency for #16 undocumented, (4) #26 Keycloak webhook guidance missing. All fixed in spec update + contracts creation.

5. Problem Solving:

   - **Parallel development with dependency isolation**: Chose 3 streams that are maximally independent — backend (Go API), frontend (Vue against mocked API), AI worker (Python gRPC). Interface contracts committed to main before agents start.
   
   - **Subagent Bash permission issue**: Critical blocker. All three streams have agents that wrote files but cannot run shell commands. Resolution: main session must handle all shell operations. - `DELETE /:kb_id` — archive KB (status=`archived`; requires workspace `admin`)
- All operations run inside `db.WithOrgID` transactions for RLS enforcement
- Slug uniqueness scoped to workspace (`UNIQUE(workspace_id, slug)`)
- 5 unit tests: create, 422, get, 404, list-empty-array, archive

Closes #27
EOF
)"
